labels.name: blocked
labels.description: Blocked by another issue or dependency

milestones.title: Sprint 1 — Foundation
milestones.description: Core auth, onboarding, and style system hardening

milestones.title: Sprint 2 — HR Core
milestones.description: Leave management, payslip, and employee profile features

issues.title: OTP code not visible in the user mail
issues.body:
## Summary

The OTP verification email is delivered successfully but the one-time code is not rendered in the email body. Recipients receive the email with no visible code, making it impossible to complete the verification step.

## Current Behavior

- User triggers OTP (registration, password reset, or 2FA)
- Email arrives in inbox with subject and layout intact
- The OTP code field is blank, missing, or shows a template placeholder (e.g. `{{otp}}`)

## Expected Behavior

- The email renders the 6-digit OTP code prominently
- The code is valid for the configured TTL (e.g. 10 minutes)
- A fallback plain-text part includes the code for clients that block HTML

## Likely Root Cause

The OTP value is not being interpolated into the email template before dispatch. Check the mailer service for missing variable binding between OTP generation and template rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OTP email renders the code correctly in HTML clients (Gmail, Outlook)
- [ ] OTP email includes a plain-text fallback with the code
- [ ] Code is valid for the configured TTL and single-use only
- [ ] Expiry and single-use invalidation are covered by unit tests

issues.title: refactor: remove all auth toast feedback, and also design a better toast ui that fit the theme of the app
issues.body:
## Current State

Authentication flows (login, OTP, forgot password, reset password) surface all feedback — including inline validation errors and API error responses — exclusively via toast notifications. This creates several UX problems:

- Errors disappear automatically before the user can act on them
- The toast position and duration interrupt the form interaction flow
- The current toast component style does not match the app's design language (colours, border radius, typography)
- Success toasts for multi-step actions like password reset give no next-step guidance

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Remove toasts from form error paths
- Inline validation errors → render under the affected field using `<FormMessage>` from React Hook Form
- API error responses (4xx) → render in a `role="alert"` error banner above the submit button
- Only retain toasts for non-blocking, system-level notifications (e.g. "Session expired — please log in again")

### 2. Redesign the `<Toast>` / `<Toaster>` component
- Variants: `success`, `error`, `warning`, `info`
- Slots: icon + title (required) + body copy (optional) + dismiss button
- Colours, border radius, and shadow derived from the app's CSS custom property token system — no hardcoded hex values
- Fully responsive in both light and dark themes

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Auth forms show field-level errors inline; no auth-flow errors appear as toasts
- [ ] New `<Toast>` component passes visual QA in both light and dark themes
- [ ] Toast has `success`, `error`, `warning`, `info` variants with correct colour tokens
- [ ] All existing non-auth toasts are migrated to the new component
- [ ] Toast container announces via `role="alert"` or `aria-live="assertive"` (WCAG 2.1 AA 4.1.3)

issues.title: refactor: improve the dark theme ui
issues.body:
## Current State

The dark theme is functional but visually inconsistent. Several components use hardcoded colours that do not adapt to the active theme, resulting in low-contrast text, invisible borders, and mismatched surface elevations.

## Known Problem Areas

| Area | Issue |
|------|-------|
| Sidebar | Background bleeds through at certain scroll positions |
| Form inputs | Border colour is barely visible on dark surfaces |
| Data tables | Alternating row shading disappears in dark mode |
| Modal overlays | Backdrop is too transparent against dark backgrounds |
| Status badges | Leave and payroll badges lose contrast in dark mode |
| Dropdown menus | Hover/active states blend into the background |

## Proposed Changes

1. **Token audit** — replace any hardcoded `#hex` or `rgb()` values in component files with CSS custom property tokens from `variables.css`
2. **Surface elevation scale** — define a consistent three-level scale (base → raised → overlay) for dark mode and apply it to sidebar, cards, modals, and dropdowns
3. **Contrast audit** — verify all text and interactive elements meet WCAG AA ratios (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text) in dark mode
4. **Interactive states** — ensure hover, focus, and active states have visible contrast in dark mode across all interactive components

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All text in dark mode meets WCAG AA contrast ratio
- [ ] No hardcoded colour values remain in component files — all reference design tokens
- [ ] Surface elevation scale is consistent across sidebar, cards, modals, and dropdowns
- [ ] Status badges (leave types, payroll status) are legible in both themes
- [ ] Form input borders and placeholders are visible in dark mode
- [ ] Automated axe-playwright audit passes in dark mode with no contrast violations

issues.title: [Auth]-Impl refresh access token
issues.body:
## Summary

The app currently issues a short-lived access token on login but has no mechanism to silently refresh it. When the token expires, the user is abruptly logged out mid-session, creating a disruptive experience and forcing a full re-login.

## Current Behavior

- Access token expires (e.g. after 15 minutes)
- The next authenticated API call returns `401 Unauthorized`
- The user is redirected to `/login` with no explanation
- Any unsaved work in progress is lost

## Proposed Changes

1. **Axios / fetch interceptor** — intercept `401` responses and attempt a silent token refresh before retrying the original request
2. **Refresh token storage** — store the refresh token in an `httpOnly` cookie (not `localStorage`) to prevent XSS exposure
3. **Token rotation** — invalidate the old refresh token on each use and issue a new pair (access + refresh)
4. **Race condition guard** — queue concurrent requests that arrive during a refresh and replay them once the new token is issued; avoid multiple simultaneous refresh calls
5. **Hard logout** — if the refresh request itself returns `401` (e.g. token revoked or expired), clear the session and redirect to `/login` with a "Session expired" message

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Access token is silently refreshed without user interruption
- [ ] Refresh token is stored in an `httpOnly` cookie
- [ ] Token rotation is enforced — old refresh tokens are invalidated on use
- [ ] Concurrent requests during refresh are queued and replayed correctly
- [ ] Expired or revoked refresh token triggers a clean logout with a user-visible message
- [ ] Unit tests cover the interceptor, queue behaviour, and hard-logout path

issues.title: [team, profile, company]- the user need a profile picture (avatar)
issues.body:
## Summary

Users and employees currently have no way to upload or display a profile picture. Avatars are absent across the team directory, employee profile page, and company roster, making it harder to identify individuals at a glance.

## Scope

This issue spans both backend (storage, API) and frontend (upload UI, display).

## Proposed Changes

### Backend
- Add a `POST /profile/avatar` endpoint that accepts `multipart/form-data`
- Validate file type (JPEG, PNG, WebP only) and size (max 2 MB)
- Store the file in object storage (S3 or equivalent) and persist the URL on the user/employee record
- Add a `DELETE /profile/avatar` endpoint to revert to the default avatar
- Return the avatar URL in all relevant profile and team member response payloads

### Frontend
- Add an avatar upload control to the profile settings page (click to upload, drag-and-drop)
- Show a circular crop preview before confirming the upload
- Display the avatar in the team directory list, sub-team detail view, employee profile header, and the top-nav user menu
- Fall back to initials-based avatar when no image is set

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User can upload a JPEG, PNG, or WebP avatar up to 2 MB from the profile settings page
- [ ] Invalid file type or oversized file shows a clear inline error message
- [ ] Avatar is displayed in team directory, sub-team view, employee profile, and nav menu
- [ ] Fallback initials avatar renders when no image is uploaded
- [ ] User can remove their avatar and revert to the fallback
- [ ] Uploaded file is stored in object storage; no binary data is persisted in the database

issues.title: [team]- user cannot view team members in the team and sub team detail view
issues.body:
## Summary

The team detail page and sub-team detail page do not render a list of members. Users navigating to a team or sub-team see the name and description but no roster, making the pages functionally empty.

## Current Behavior

- Navigate to **Teams → [Team Name]** or **Teams → [Team Name] → [Sub-team Name]**
- The member list section is blank (empty state, spinner, or missing section entirely)
- No network error is visible in the UI, but the member data may not be fetched

## Expected Behavior

- The detail view fetches and displays all members of the selected team or sub-team
- Each member row shows: avatar, full name, job title, and role within the team (e.g. Lead, Member)
- An empty state with a prompt to add members is shown when the team has no members

## Likely Root Cause

The team detail query either does not include a `members` relation in the response payload, or the frontend is not consuming the members field from the API response.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Team detail view lists all members with avatar, name, title, and team role
- [ ] Sub-team detail view lists all members independently of the parent team
- [ ] Empty state is shown when a team has no members
- [ ] Member list is paginated or virtualized for teams with 50+ members
- [ ] API response for team detail includes a `members` array with the required fields

issues.title: [team]-cannot delete a team or a sub team
issues.body:
## Summary

Attempting to delete a team or sub-team results in a `500 Internal Server Error`. The operation fails silently on the frontend, and the team/sub-team remains in the list.

## Current Behavior

- User clicks **Delete** on a team or sub-team
- The API returns:
  ```json
  { "success": false, "message": "internal server error" }
  ```
- The UI may show no feedback, or a generic error toast
- The team/sub-team is not removed

## Expected Behavior

- Team or sub-team is soft-deleted (or hard-deleted per business rules)
- Members are unlinked or reassigned according to business logic
- A confirmation dialog is shown before deletion
- The item is removed from the list immediately on success (optimistic update)

## Investigation Checklist

- [ ] Check the server logs for the root exception thrown during delete
- [ ] Verify the delete handler handles FK constraint violations (members still linked)
- [ ] Confirm the cascade or unlink strategy for child sub-teams and members
- [ ] Check whether soft-delete (`deletedAt`) is expected but the query does not set it

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Deleting a team with no members succeeds and removes the item from the list
- [ ] Deleting a team with members either reassigns or unlinks them per business rules — no FK constraint error
- [ ] Deleting a sub-team does not affect the parent team or sibling sub-teams
- [ ] A confirmation dialog is required before deletion
- [ ] The server returns `200 OK` (or `204 No Content`) on success
- [ ] The server returns a structured error (not 500) if deletion is blocked by a business rule

issues.title: [modules]-impl all download CTA
issues.body:
## Summary

Several modules expose "Download" buttons or links in the UI but they are not wired to any download logic. Clicking them has no effect, which makes the feature appear broken to users.

## Affected Modules

Audit the following views and confirm which download CTAs are unimplemented:

| Module | Asset | Expected Format |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| Payslip | Monthly payslip | PDF |
| Leave | Leave summary / history export | CSV or PDF |
| Employee profile | Employee data export | CSV or PDF |
| Payroll | Payroll run report | PDF or XLSX |

## Proposed Approach

1. For server-generated documents (payslips, reports): call a backend endpoint that returns a file stream; trigger a browser download via a temporary object URL
2. For client-side exports (CSV from table data): generate the file in the browser using a library such as `papaparse` and trigger a download
3. Show a loading indicator on the button while the file is being generated
4. Handle and display errors if the download fails

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All identified download CTAs trigger a file download when clicked
- [ ] A loading state is visible on the button during generation
- [ ] Downloaded file names are descriptive (e.g. `payslip-march-2026.pdf`, `leave-history-2026.csv`)
- [ ] Errors during download are surfaced to the user with an actionable message
- [ ] Downloads work correctly in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari

issues.title: [leave]-the leave hub view is broken, showing a 404 api response
issues.body:
## Summary

The Leave Hub page fails to load and displays a 404 error. Users are unable to view their leave balance, history, or submit new leave requests.

## Current Behavior

- User navigates to the Leave Hub
- The page renders a 404 error state (from the API response)
- Leave balance, upcoming leaves, and history sections are all empty or hidden
- No actionable error message is shown to guide the user

## Expected Behavior

- The Leave Hub loads successfully and displays the current user's leave balance by type
- Upcoming approved leaves and recent history are listed
- The "Apply for Leave" CTA is accessible

## Investigation Checklist

- [ ] Confirm the API endpoint path used by the frontend matches the backend route definition
- [ ] Check whether the route requires a specific path param (e.g. `employeeId`) that is missing or malformed
- [ ] Verify that the authenticated user's employee record exists and is linked to their account
- [ ] Check for a recent backend route rename or restructure that was not reflected in the frontend

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Leave Hub loads without errors for all authenticated employees
- [ ] Leave balances are displayed per leave type (annual, sick, personal, etc.)
- [ ] If no leave records exist, a meaningful empty state is shown
- [ ] The API returns `200 OK` with the correct payload shape
- [ ] Error boundary catches unexpected failures and shows a user-friendly message instead of a blank page

issues.title: [payroll]-setup modal still shows up after setup completed
issues.body:
## Summary

After a user completes the payroll setup flow, the setup modal continues to appear on subsequent visits. The completed state is not persisted or not being read correctly on mount, causing the modal to re-surface unnecessarily.

## Current Behavior

- Admin completes all steps of the payroll setup wizard
- On navigating away and returning to the Payroll module, the setup modal opens again
- The completed setup data is present (payroll is functional), but the modal ignores it

## Expected Behavior

- Once payroll setup is marked complete, the modal never reappears for that organization
- The payroll dashboard renders directly without the setup gate

## Likely Root Cause

The setup completion flag is either not being persisted to the backend, or the frontend is not fetching/reading it correctly on mount. Possible causes:
- The completion flag is stored in local/session state only and lost on navigation
- The API check for setup status returns an incorrect or uncached response
- The modal visibility condition checks a field that is not updated after the final step

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Setup modal does not appear after initial setup is completed
- [ ] Setup completion state is persisted on the backend (not client-only)
- [ ] Refreshing the page or navigating away and returning does not re-trigger the modal
- [ ] If setup is genuinely incomplete, the modal still appears correctly
- [ ] An admin can re-open setup settings via an explicit "Edit Setup" action (not by the modal auto-appearing)

issues.title: [payroll]-dev environment payroll workflow not working properly
issues.body:
## Summary

The end-to-end payroll workflow is broken in the development environment. The wallet balance does not update after a funding operation, which blocks all downstream steps (payroll run, disbursement). This makes it impossible for developers to test the full payroll flow locally.

## Current Behavior

- Admin initiates a wallet funding action in the dev environment
- The funding request appears to succeed (no error returned)
- The wallet balance remains unchanged
- Subsequent payroll run steps that depend on sufficient balance fail or are blocked

## Temporary Workaround

Hardcode a sufficient wallet balance in the dev environment seed data or mock service to unblock payroll workflow testing while the root cause is investigated.

## Investigation Checklist

- [ ] Confirm whether the wallet funding webhook/callback is being received in the dev environment
- [ ] Check if the payment provider (Paystack, Flutterwave, etc.) sandbox is configured correctly for dev
- [ ] Verify that the balance update handler is triggered after a successful funding event
- [ ] Check for env-specific feature flags or guards that suppress the balance update in non-production environments

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Wallet balance updates correctly after a funding operation in the dev environment
- [ ] The full payroll workflow (fund → run payroll → disburse) completes end-to-end in dev
- [ ] Dev environment uses sandbox credentials that correctly simulate payment callbacks
- [ ] A seed script or dev fixture provides a pre-funded wallet so developers can test payroll without depending on the payment provider sandbox

issues.title: [payslip]-an employee cannot view there monthly salary
issues.body:
## Summary

Employees have no way to view their monthly salary or payslip through the app. The payslip module UI exists but the underlying data endpoint is missing, leaving the page empty or in a perpetual loading state.

## Current Behavior

- Employee navigates to the Payslip module
- No salary or payslip data is displayed
- The API either has no endpoint for employee salary/payslip retrieval, or the endpoint does not return the required fields

## Expected Behavior

- Employee can view a list of monthly payslips sorted by date (most recent first)
- Each payslip entry shows: pay period, gross pay, deductions (tax, pension, etc.), and net pay
- Employee can drill into a payslip to see a full breakdown
- Employee can download the payslip as a PDF

## Required Backend Changes

- `GET /payslip` — return a paginated list of payslips for the authenticated employee
- `GET /payslip/:id` — return the full breakdown for a single payslip
- Each payslip record must include: `payPeriod`, `grossPay`, `deductions[]`, `netPay`, `status`, `generatedAt`
- Alternatively, if an employee profile endpoint exists, confirm it exposes `currentSalary` and payslip history

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /payslip` returns a paginated list of payslips for the authenticated employee
- [ ] `GET /payslip/:id` returns a full payslip breakdown
- [ ] The payslip list page renders correctly with real data
- [ ] Each payslip shows gross pay, itemised deductions, and net pay
- [ ] Employee can download a payslip as a PDF
- [ ] An empty state is shown if no payslips have been generated yet
- [ ] Endpoint is protected — employees can only access their own payslips
